comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Implemented in 9a688aa8. Extracted _resolve_session(tracker) in transition.py (chain REBAR_SESSION_ID -> SESSION_ID -> _short_head -> 'unknown'); documented the additive (no-deprecation-warning) precedence in docs/config.md Back-compat section; added 4 precedence tests. Verified: pytest -k session (5 passed), make lint clean, force-close tests pass, grep REBAR_SESSION_ID docs/config.md.

compiled_state.description:
## What
Implement the approved "support both" SESSION_ID disposition (decided on 83f2, dull-strife-sigma): event provenance reads `REBAR_SESSION_ID` (explicit, rebar-owned) with precedence, falling back to the ambient `SESSION_ID`, then short git HEAD, then "unknown". Additive + backward-compatible — users who set only ambient `SESSION_ID` see no behavior change; `REBAR_SESSION_ID` gives an explicit, non-colliding override.

Note on the alias-window convention (clarifies plan-review finding): this is an ADDITIVE "support both" precedence, NOT a deprecating rename. Ambient `SESSION_ID` remains permanently valid, so — unlike the 60ce config-key renames routed through config.py with a deprecation warning — this is a plain silent `os.environ` fallback chain with NO deprecation warning. Documented as such.

## Files
- `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py`: the session-provenance read (currently line 510, inside the `force_close` audit-comment block only — this provenance is scoped to the FORCE_CLOSE audit comment, not all transitions). Extract a testable `_resolve_session(tracker)` helper implementing the chain `REBAR_SESSION_ID` -> `SESSION_ID` -> `_short_head` -> "unknown".
- `docs/config.md`: document `REBAR_SESSION_ID` / `SESSION_ID` precedence (additive, no deprecation warning) near the Back-compat env-var section.
- `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_completion_gate.py` (or a sibling): unit-test the precedence chain via the extracted helper.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Provenance reads `REBAR_SESSION_ID` with precedence over ambient `SESSION_ID` (chain: `REBAR_SESSION_ID` -> `SESSION_ID` -> short git HEAD -> "unknown")
- [ ] Backward-compatible: setting only ambient `SESSION_ID` behaves exactly as before
- [ ] Docs updated to document the precedence + that it is additive (no deprecation warning)
- [ ] Four test cases pass: (a) REBAR_SESSION_ID set (wins even when SESSION_ID also set), (b) only SESSION_ID set, (c) neither set but git HEAD available, (d) neither set and no HEAD -> "unknown"

## Verification
- AC1/AC2/AC4: `pytest tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_completion_gate.py -k session -v`
- AC3: `grep -n REBAR_SESSION_ID docs/config.md`
compiled_state.title: Add REBAR_SESSION_ID alias (support both) — provenance precedence over ambient SESSION_ID
